steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. Harris tells an ane\cdote about a rough Channel crossing — but notice that who else stayed well keeps changing: first the captain, then the second mate, then 'one other man,' then possibly no one. The one constant is that Harris himself is always the healthy one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Harris is worried that the captain will start to feel sick.': The passage says nothing about worry or a current trip. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Harris has a hard time remembering when his friends ask.': No friends ask him anything in the passage; the shifting details look deliberate, not like a memory lapse. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Harris often changes the details but always brags about his own wellness.': Exactly what the passage shows — the companion changes every time, yet Harris is always well. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Harris spent most of his time with a particular passenger.': The passage never identifies a single passenger; the companion keeps changing. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
